name: Dialog
description: A general-purpose modal overlay for displaying content, forms, or interactive flows. Dismissible via backdrop click or Escape. Supports Header/Panel/Footer layout, scroll indicators, nested stacking, and an optional close button. Built on Base UI Dialog with StyleX styling.

Root.description: State container. Renders no DOM element.

defaultOpen.description: Initial open state for uncontrolled mode.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the dialog opens or closes.

onOpenChangeComplete.description: Callback fired after open/close animations complete.

dismissible.description: Whether the dialog closes on backdrop click or Escape key.

Trigger.description: Button that opens the dialog. Unstyled by default — use the render prop to swap in a styled Button.

render.description: Replace the underlying element. Use to render a styled Button.

dataAttributes.data-popup-open: Present when the dialog is open.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the trigger is disabled.

Portal.description: Renders children in a React portal outside the DOM tree.

keepMounted.description: Keep in DOM when closed. Pass true if you need exit animations and handle closed-state visibility in CSS.

container.description: Target parent element for the portal.

Backdrop.description: Semi-transparent overlay behind the dialog. Clicking it closes the dialog (unless dismissible is false). Has fade animation via global CSS.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the dialog is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the dialog is closed.

Popup.description: Centered card containing the dialog content. Viewport is wrapped internally for centering and scroll management. Includes an optional close button (X icon) in the top-right corner.

showCloseButton.description: Whether to show the X close button in the top-right corner.

initialFocus.description: Element to focus when the dialog opens.

finalFocus.description: Element to focus when the dialog closes.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the dialog is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the dialog is closed.
dataAttributes.data-nested: Present when nested inside another dialog.
dataAttributes.data-nested-dialog-open: Present when a child dialog is open.

Header.description: Container for Title and Description. Provides consistent padding above the scrollable Panel. Custom part not in Base UI.

Title.description: Dialog heading. Automatically wired to aria-labelledby.

Description.description: Dialog body text below the title. Automatically wired to aria-describedby.

Panel.description: Scrollable content area between Header and Footer. Uses data attributes and inset box-shadow for scroll indicators. Custom part not in Base UI.

dataAttributes.data-scroll-top: Present when content is scrolled away from the top (more content above).
dataAttributes.data-scroll-bottom: Present when there is more content below the visible area.

Footer.description: Flex row for action buttons (Cancel/Submit/Done). Custom part not in Base UI.

variant.description: Visual style. 'default' has no top border, 'bordered' adds a separator line.

Close.description: Button that closes the dialog on click. Unstyled — use the render prop to swap in a styled Button.

render.description: Replace the underlying element. Use to render a styled Button.

cssRequirements.description: Backdrop fade, popup scale, nested stagger, and scroll indicator animations require global CSS rules inside @layer priority1. StyleX cannot target data-attribute selectors, so these rules must live in the consumer's global stylesheet.

intents.reasoning: Dialog is the right component for displaying focused informational content in an overlay that the user can easily dismiss.
intents.composition:
<Dialog.Root>
  <Dialog.Trigger render={<Button variant="outline">View details</Button>} />
  <Dialog.Portal>
    <Dialog.Backdrop />
    <Dialog.Popup>
      <Dialog.Header>
        <Dialog.Title>Item details</Dialog.Title>
        <Dialog.Description>Detail content here.</Dialog.Description>
      </Dialog.Header>
      <Dialog.Panel>
        {/* detail content */}
      </Dialog.Panel>
      <Dialog.Footer>
        <Dialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Close</Button>} />
      </Dialog.Footer>
    </Dialog.Popup>
  </Dialog.Portal>
</Dialog.Root>

intents.reasoning: Dialog provides modal focus for form interactions, preventing accidental clicks outside while the user is editing.
intents.composition:
<Dialog.Root open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <Dialog.Trigger render={<Button>Edit</Button>} />
  <Dialog.Portal>
    <Dialog.Backdrop />
    <Dialog.Popup>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} style={{ display: 'contents' }}>
        <Dialog.Header>
          <Dialog.Title>Edit item</Dialog.Title>
          <Dialog.Description>Update the fields below.</Dialog.Description>
        </Dialog.Header>
        <Dialog.Panel>
          {/* form fields */}
        </Dialog.Panel>
        <Dialog.Footer>
          <Dialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>} />
          <Button type="submit">Save</Button>
        </Dialog.Footer>
      </form>
    </Dialog.Popup>
  </Dialog.Portal>
</Dialog.Root>

intents.reasoning: Dialog works well for focused settings interactions that benefit from modal isolation without the forced-acknowledgment behaviour of AlertDialog.
intents.composition:
<Dialog.Root>
  <Dialog.Trigger render={<Button variant="outline">Settings</Button>} />
  <Dialog.Portal>
    <Dialog.Backdrop />
    <Dialog.Popup>
      <Dialog.Header>
        <Dialog.Title>Settings</Dialog.Title>
        <Dialog.Description>Configure your preferences.</Dialog.Description>
      </Dialog.Header>
      <Dialog.Panel>
        {/* settings controls */}
      </Dialog.Panel>
      <Dialog.Footer>
        <Dialog.Close render={<Button>Done</Button>} />
      </Dialog.Footer>
    </Dialog.Popup>
  </Dialog.Portal>
</Dialog.Root>

avoidWhen.reasoning: Use AlertDialog instead. Dialog can be dismissed by clicking the backdrop, which is too easy to accidentally close for critical confirmations.

avoidWhen.scenario: Non-critical transient feedback or status updates
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Toast for ephemeral messages. Dialog demands too much attention for simple feedback.

avoidWhen.scenario: Selecting from a list of options
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Select or Combobox for option selection. Dialog is overweight for a simple pick-one interaction.

avoidWhen.scenario: Content that should remain visible alongside the page
avoidWhen.reasoning: Dialog blocks the page behind an overlay. Use Collapsible or inline expansion for content that should coexist with the page.

examples.name: form
examples.description: Form dialog with scrollable content, submit and cancel
examples.code:
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

<Dialog.Root open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <Dialog.Trigger render={<Button>Edit profile</Button>} />
  <Dialog.Portal>
    <Dialog.Backdrop />
    <Dialog.Popup>
      <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} style={{ display: 'contents' }}>
        <Dialog.Header>
          <Dialog.Title>Edit profile</Dialog.Title>
          <Dialog.Description>Update your display name and bio.</Dialog.Description>
        </Dialog.Header>
        <Dialog.Panel>
          {/* form fields */}
        </Dialog.Panel>
        <Dialog.Footer>
          <Dialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>} />
          <Button type="submit">Save</Button>
        </Dialog.Footer>
      </form>
    </Dialog.Popup>
  </Dialog.Portal>
</Dialog.Root>

examples.name: settings
examples.code:
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

<Dialog.Root open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <Dialog.Trigger render={<Button variant="outline">Settings</Button>} />
  <Dialog.Portal>
    <Dialog.Backdrop />
    <Dialog.Popup>
      <Dialog.Header>
        <Dialog.Title>Notification settings</Dialog.Title>
        <Dialog.Description>Choose which notifications you receive.</Dialog.Description>
      </Dialog.Header>
      <Dialog.Panel>
        <CheckboxGroup.Root>
          {/* checkbox items */}
        </CheckboxGroup.Root>
      </Dialog.Panel>
      <Dialog.Footer>
        <Dialog.Close render={<Button>Done</Button>} />
      </Dialog.Footer>
    </Dialog.Popup>
  </Dialog.Portal>
</Dialog.Root>

examples.name: nested
examples.code:
<Dialog.Root>
  <Dialog.Trigger render={<Button>Open</Button>} />
  <Dialog.Portal>
    <Dialog.Backdrop />
    <Dialog.Popup>
      <Dialog.Header>
        <Dialog.Title>Parent dialog</Dialog.Title>
        <Dialog.Description>This dialog can open another dialog on top.</Dialog.Description>
      </Dialog.Header>
      <Dialog.Footer>
        <Dialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Close</Button>} />
        <Dialog.Root>
          <Dialog.Trigger render={<Button variant="outline">Open nested</Button>} />
          <Dialog.Portal>
            <Dialog.Backdrop />
            <Dialog.Popup>
              <Dialog.Header>
                <Dialog.Title>Nested dialog</Dialog.Title>
                <Dialog.Description>This is a nested dialog.</Dialog.Description>
              </Dialog.Header>
              <Dialog.Footer>
                <Dialog.Close render={<Button>Close</Button>} />
              </Dialog.Footer>
            </Dialog.Popup>
          </Dialog.Portal>
        </Dialog.Root>
      </Dialog.Footer>
    </Dialog.Popup>
  </Dialog.Portal>
</Dialog.Root>
